Mantralaya, Mumbai 400032.
Date: June 12, 2026.
The Government Order:
Regulation of transfers and discharge of government duties of Maharashtra Government employees.
As per the provisions of Section 5, the Superintending Engineer in the Water Resources Department is mentioned in the following table:
Officers of the (Architectural / Mechanical) cadre for administrative reasons and of the Civil Service Board.
With the approval of the competent authority as per the recommendation, the replacement post will be posted at Column No. 4 as shown here.
It is being done: -

para_trans: Government Order No: LTS 2026 / P.No.118 / A (Class-I)
2. The officers transferred and posted under the said order shall be ex-officio liable under this order.
Relaxation is being done and it is about redeployment of these officers at the field level.
There is no need to execute the command. Accordingly transferred by the concerned Superintendent Engineer.
Join immediately at the place of posting.
3. The date on which the officer concerned was transferred and appeared at the place of posting.
The government should be notified immediately on the doesttclass1.wrd@maharashtra.gov.in e-mail website.
4. The officer concerned in the position being transferred by virtue of the said order.
On joining, the additional charge of the post concerned is transferred to another officer under the previous order.
If assigned, it will automatically terminate.
5. Leave granted to concerned officers for any reason without prior approval of the Government.
Don't do it. Also transferred by original office as per Rule-30 (c) of M.N.S. (Leave) Rules, 1981.
No leave applications of the incumbent should be accepted. If such an application is received, it shall be
Payments must be made by post to the relevant officer at the original address. Transfer to the original office
If the officer's leave application is sent, these charges will not be taken into account.
6. failing to appear at the place of immediate appointment in accordance with the said transfer order or any
If an attempt is made to cancel the transfer under duress, the action is taken by the Maharashtra Civil Service (Conduct).
Being in violation of Rule 23 of the Rules 1979, it is considered misconduct against them.
Those concerned should note that disciplinary action will be taken.
